page: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine configured with 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The underlying hardware features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. This processor contains 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. The instance includes 472 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a significant divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core capabilities are highly competitive, with the Geekbench single-core score of 2170.0 placing in the top tier (top 10%) and single-threaded gzip compression reaching a strong 31.35 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression are categorized as weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for read-only large blocks is also weak at 37.14 GB/sec, while write bandwidth for large blocks remains average at 25.89 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E8-4ds_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. The inclusion of 472 GB of local storage adds value for applications requiring fast local scratch space. Given the strong single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling, this server is best suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, caching layers, and lightweight web serving rather than highly parallelized compilation or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Edsv6 family, featuring 4 vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 472 GB of local SSD storage. The server exhibits strong single-core performance, highlighted by a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2170.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core performance and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng multi-core and large-block read benchmarks. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core, this instance provides qualitative resource density for memory-bound applications. It is best suited for database operations, text processing, and caching workloads that benefit from high memory capacity and strong single-thread execution rather than heavy multi-threaded parallel processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v6 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage. It delivers top-tier single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4ds_v6 is an Edsv6 VM with 4 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, offering strong single-core Intel Xeon performance.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high single-core speed, substantial RAM density, and integrated local SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory density
472 GB of integrated local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression
Weaker multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth tradeoffs
Best suited for database operations, text processing, and caching layers
